title: U.K. government preparing to release new space strategy
summary: The U.K. government is set to unveil a new space strategy that emphasizes a coordinated, government-wide approach to space activities. This initiative aims to streamline and enhance the country's space sector policies and operations.

title: Flexell Space raises $20 Million in Series A round
summary: Flexell Space, a developer of next-generation space solar solutions, raised $20 million in a Series A funding round. The company is based in Ulsan, South Korea.

title: Defense spending lifts Europe’s space economy
summary: European government space spending increased by 12% to $15.4 billion in 2025, driven by rising defense budgets, despite a 3% global decline. This trend highlights the growing role of defense in Europe's space economy.

title: L3Harris, Sierra Space to build 36 satellites to expand U.S. missile-tracking network
summary: L3Harris and Sierra Space have been awarded a combined $1.75 billion contract by the Space Development Agency to build 36 satellites for expanding the U.S. missile-tracking network.

title: Poland to host new ESA center as government boosts investments in space
summary: Poland will establish the first European Space Agency (ESA) center in an eastern flank member state, focusing on civil security and resilience. The move aligns with Poland's increased investments in the space sector.

title: Accel and Prosus backed QOSMIC raises $3.33 Million to Build Optical Ground Stations for the Orbital Data Economy
summary: QOSMIC raised $3.33 million in seed funding led by Accel and Prosus to develop optical ground stations for the orbital data economy. The investment aims to enhance data transmission capabilities between space and Earth.

title: Reditus Space completes first reentry vehicle
summary: Reditus Space, a startup focused on recoverable and reusable spacecraft, has completed its first reentry vehicle. The vehicle is scheduled for launch later this year.

title: Voyager completes acquisition of Astrobotic
summary: Voyager Technologies has finalized its acquisition of Astrobotic Technology, a lunar infrastructure company. The deal follows Astrobotic's recent selection for two NASA lunar lander missions.

title: Getting ready for lunar planetary defense
summary: NASA unveiled plans for a new moon base, marking a significant step toward establishing a permanent human presence on the Moon. This development is part of broader efforts in lunar planetary defense and space exploration.

title: China lines up methalox Long March 10C as commercial workhorse after first booster recovery
summary: China has designated the methalox Long March 10C as a commercial workhorse after achieving its first successful booster recovery. The move follows additional capital infusion into commercial rocket ventures.

title: How to watch SpaceX launch Starship Flight 13 on July 16
summary: SpaceX is preparing for the 13th test flight of its Starship on July 16, which will include the first deployment of Starlink V3 satellites and test booster recovery and upgraded flight systems. This marks a significant step toward operational missions for the Starship program.

title: Moon landings could destroy evidence of life's origins
summary: Moon landings may inadvertently destroy evidence of life's origins, prompting concerns about preserving scientific data and investments in space exploration.

title: SpaceX Starship Flight 13 launch updates: SpaceX sets target date for next Starship rocket launch
summary: SpaceX has set a target date for the next launch of its Starship rocket, marking another step in its testing program. The company continues to advance toward the thirteenth flight of the Starship system.

title: These mysterious exoplanets may have clouds of vaporized rock and grounds of scorching magma oceans
summary: Sub-Neptune exoplanets may have atmospheres dense enough to vaporize rock, forming clouds and trapping heat over magma oceans. This discovery expands our understanding of extreme planetary conditions.

title: Russia's launching a NASA astronaut and 2 cosmonauts to the International Space Station on July 14: Watch it live
summary: Russia is launching a NASA astronaut and two cosmonauts to the International Space Station on July 14. The event will be broadcast live.

title: NASA begins stacking rocket ahead of 2027 Artemis III astronaut launch (photos)
summary: NASA has started stacking the solid rocket booster segments for the SLS, which will support the Artemis III astronaut mission planned for 2027. The assembly marks a key milestone ahead of the lunar landing mission.

title: SpaceX targets July 16 for Starship Flight 13, reveals what went wrong on previous launch
summary: SpaceX has scheduled the 13th flight of its Starship rocket for no earlier than July 16, following the mostly successful Flight 12. The upcoming launch will use the Version 3 configuration, similar to the previous flight.

title: "The Ninth Jedi" is a bold new Star Wars anime project hitting Disney+ next month, and it looks sick!
summary: The Ninth Jedi is a new Star Wars anime series set to premiere on Disney+ next month, expanding on a 2021 episode from Star Wars: Visions directed by Kenji Kamiyama.

title: Extremely Large Telescope reaches a major milestone | Space photo of the day for July 13, 2026
summary: The Extremely Large Telescope has achieved a significant milestone, as highlighted in a space photo from July 13, 2026. The image showcases the telescope's impressive size and progress.

title: Fans of the Arctic
summary: The news describes a geological feature on Russia's Severny Island where sediment from ice-capped mountains spreads across a river valley. This is a natural phenomenon unrelated to the space industry.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4941-4947: (Pin)Stripes on the Fourth of July
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover approached a geologic boundary between a smooth sandy region and rougher bedrock ahead of the Fourth of July holiday. The rover observed polygonal features while planning its next activities.

title: NASA Study Points to Smoother Air Taxi Rides
summary: NASA research aims to improve passenger comfort in air taxis, a key factor for the emerging industry's growth. The study explores how ride smoothness influences willingness to fly.

title: NASA Astronaut Anil Menon
summary: NASA astronaut Anil Menon is set to launch aboard the Roscosmos Soyuz MS-29 spacecraft to the International Space Station on July 14, 2026, alongside cosmonauts Pyotr Dubrov and Anna Kikina.

title: NASA’s Hubble Discovers First of Star Cluster’s Missing Black Holes
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope, with data from Webb, has discovered the first stellar-mass black hole in the star cluster Omega Centauri. This finding helps fill a gap in understanding black hole populations in star clusters.

title: Early Career Faculty (ECF) 2025 Awards
summary: NASA's Early Career Faculty (ECF) 2025 Awards focus on advanced diagnostics for spacecraft entry simulations and autonomous spacecraft guidance using machine learning. The awards support innovative research to enhance space exploration technologies.

title: NASA Volunteers Help Zooniverse Reach 1 Billion Classifications
summary: NASA volunteers have helped Zooniverse, a NASA grantee, reach 1 billion classifications through its online research platform. This milestone highlights the global contributions of volunteers in scientific research.

title: NASA Photographer Captures Images from F-18 Over Washington
summary: NASA photographers captured unique aerial images of the agency's missions from an F-18 over Washington, providing rare public insights into NASA's research and operations.

title: Waxing Gibbous Moon
summary: The image shows the waxing gibbous moon from the International Space Station, located 264 miles above the Indian Ocean southeast of Madagascar on June 26, 2026. This lunar phase occurs before the full moon, making the moon appear brighter.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is preparing for its 13th flight test. Seven launches are scheduled worldwide this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key milestone ahead of its planned launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: SpaceX completed a record-duration static fire test of Booster 20's 33 engines, marking a significant milestone in the company's development efforts.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays. The company reported significant gains in Electron launches during this period.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant infrastructure growth with new launch pads being developed for Stoke Space and Relativity Space. This marks another step in the region's ongoing revitalization.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved multiple milestones. The station represents a key step in commercializing low Earth orbit.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress toward the milestone.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative. The contracts aim to support future lunar missions and establish a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent anomaly that severely damaged the facility during the New Glenn launch. The company aims to restore operations swiftly to support future missions.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the CM25 ministerial meeting, informing industry about planned activities and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new advanced robotic arm designed for space applications. This technology aims to enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks a return to routine science operations for the mission.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme enables scientific research in space, with its latest success being the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission launch in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to endure space conditions, supported by young researchers contributing innovative ideas.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on robust or resilient space technology, though specific details are not provided.

title: Spinifex Space Launches With Australian Test Ranges
summary: Spinifex Space has launched operations to offer end-to-end suborbital launch campaigns, private range access, and test and evaluation infrastructure. The company will utilize Australian test ranges for its services.

title: Lunar Outpost Joins ESA’s Moonraker Mission in 2030
summary: Lunar Outpost Europe will contribute thermal management solutions for ESA's Moonraker mission, which is planned for 2030. The collaboration aims to help the spacecraft endure extreme temperature variations in lunar orbit.

title: Reditus Finishes ENOS Reentry Vehicle Ahead of First Flight
summary: Reditus has completed the development of its first reentry spacecraft, ENOS, ahead of its planned debut flight this fall.

title: Reflect Orbital Cleared to Fly In-Orbit Mirror by FCC
summary: Reflect Orbital has received FCC approval to launch an in-orbit mirror despite concerns about its environmental and astronomical impact. The FCC stated that such concerns fall outside its regulatory scope.

title: Pulse Space Lands $40M Space Force Contract to Develop Laser Tech
summary: Pulse Space, a Washington-based startup, secured a $40 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to develop laser technology for beaming power to satellites in orbit. This funding will advance their mission to enable in-space energy transfer and satellite servicing.

title: US Government Studying Offshore Launch Opportunities
summary: The US Bureau of Ocean Energy Management (BOEM) issued a Request for Information (RFI) to explore building or repurposing offshore infrastructure on the Outer Continental Shelf for space launch and reentry operations.

title: NATO Allies Plan to Stitch Together Sovereign Space Fleets
summary: Eight NATO allies are planning to integrate their national defense satellites into a unified network. This initiative aims to enhance collective security and space-based defense capabilities.

title: Blue Origin Seeks $10B at $130B Valuation, Reporting Shows
summary: Blue Origin is seeking $10 billion in funding at a $130 billion valuation, marking a shift from Jeff Bezos' personal funding. The move signals the company's transition from a pet project to a more financially independent entity.

title: Auxilium Demos Tech To Enable Sustainable Bioprinting in Orbit
summary: Auxilium Biotechnologies successfully created multiple types of organ tissues aboard the ISS, demonstrating scalable bioprinting technology in orbit. This milestone highlights potential future applications for sustainable bioprinting in space.

title: ispace Launches Lunar Integrator Service With Starship
summary: ispace announced securing 500 kg of payload capacity on SpaceX's Starship for lunar missions, targeting launches no earlier than 2030. This marks the company's new Lunar Integrator Service.

title: L3Harris and Sierra Space Receive $1.75B for SDA Tranche 3, Tracking Layer
summary: L3Harris Technologies and Sierra Space secured $1.75 billion in contracts for the SDA Tranche 3, Tracking Layer satellites. The funds will enable an expedited launch under fixed price Other Transaction Authority (OTA) rapid prototyping contracts.

title: The Implications of Dual-Use for Protecting Space Assets
summary: The article discusses the evolving implications of dual-use capabilities in space, challenging traditional distinctions between civil, defense, and commercial sectors in Europe. It highlights the blurring lines between sovereign capabilities and industry-provided services.

title: Ravee Optics Secures $6 Million Seed Round to Build Compact Laser Terminals
summary: Ravee Optics, a startup developing compact laser terminals, has secured $6 million in an oversubscribed seed funding round. The company aims to advance its technology for space applications.

title: Sonatel Taps Eutelsat OneWeb for Teleport Upgrade
summary: Sonatel, Senegal's leading telecommunications company, has upgraded its teleport in collaboration with Eutelsat OneWeb. The upgrade aims to enhance connectivity services in a country with over 19 million people.

title: FCC Approves Reflect Orbital Demo Satellite to Test Redirecting Sunlight
summary: The FCC approved Reflect Orbital's demonstration satellite to test redirecting sunlight from orbit back to Earth. This marks a step toward potential commercial applications of space-based solar reflectors.

title: Space42 and Skylo Test D2D Test on Thuraya-4
summary: Space42 and Skylo conducted direct-to-device (D2D) testing on the Thuraya-4 satellite, successfully demonstrating SMS and SOS messaging. The partners announced their plans to roll out the technology following the test.

title: Ovzon’s GEO Connectivity and Terminals Complete Arctic Tests
summary: Ovzon has successfully completed Arctic trials to test its GEO connectivity and terminals in extreme environments. The company conducted three High North expeditions to validate its technology.

title: Simera Sense to Supply Imagers for Canary Islands Satellite Constellation
summary: Simera Sense will supply multispectral imagers for the Canary Islands satellite constellation project. Telespazio Ibérica has selected Simera Sense as the provider.

title: MDA Space Vertically Integrates in EO Analytics With Deal to Acquire Majority of CLS
summary: MDA Space is acquiring a majority stake in CLS, a French Earth observation data analytics company, to expand its vertical integration in EO analytics. This deal strengthens MDA Space's capabilities in satellite data processing and insights.

title: SpaceX Files for 100,000-Satellite Gen-3 Constellation
summary: SpaceX has filed with the FCC to launch a Gen-3 constellation of 100,000 satellites aimed at providing multi-gigabit connectivity. The filing includes plans to utilize W- and D-band frequencies.
